title: Friday Sustained Anchor + FTP Read
objective: Anchor the week's sustained-power load AND collect a clean FTP read: a front-loaded ~28-min sustained block tests whether 260 W is repeatable, and the smooth early ramp + steady effort give a valid DFA a1 (Polar H10) threshold read before cardiac drift sets in.
why_now: Friday is the week's key bike stimulus and the athlete prefers to use it for the FTP estimation rather than a separate test. Front-loading the sustained effort keeps the durability purpose while putting the estimation data in the first ~45 min, the only window where DFA a1 is trustworthy. FTP 260 W is athlete-confirmed but unvalidated by a long effort; this reads it without a maximal test.

targets.notes: Wear the Polar H10 and ride smooth/steady (no surging) for the first 45 min so the R-R data is clean for DFA a1. Sustained block holds 96-100% FTP (250-260 W) for ~28 min, RPE capped at 7 - honest, not maximal. This is a read, not a race.

warmup.notes: Smooth, continuous ramp from easy to high tempo. Keep it gradual and steady - this clean ramp is also the DFA a1 aerobic-threshold read.

steps.notes: Settle into sweet spot. Even power, relaxed breathing, find a rhythm before lifting.

The read: hold 250-260 W steady for the full 28 min. Smooth, no surges. Cap RPE at 7 - if it climbs to 8 or power starts sagging, hold the low end (250) and finish clean. Note whether the last 5 min felt the same as the first 5.
Easy spin to recover from the block before the endurance work.
Durability endurance - the anchor's main aerobic purpose. Smooth Z2, fuel steadily, keep cadence comfortable.

nutrition.pre: Carb-forward meal 2-4h before, ~1-2g carbs/kg. This is a long, meaningful ride - start topped up.
nutrition.during: 40-60g carbs/hour from the sustained block onward; start drinking early. Good fueling protects both the block quality and the endurance back half.
nutrition.post: 25-35g protein plus 60-90g carbs within an hour; it's a big day before the weekend.
nutrition.centr_recipe: No new Centr link needed; a normal carb+protein recovery plate works.
nutrition.supplements: Today's workout needs: optional caffeine 1-3 mg/kg 30-60 min before if tolerated and not late in the day. Daily baseline only if already routine.

recovery.post_session: Refuel promptly, fluids to thirst, legs-up or easy walk if stiff.
recovery.evening: Protect sleep; it's the input to a clean wellness reading tomorrow.
recovery.next_morning_check: Note legs, sleep, HRV/resting HR before any weekend plans.

skill_focus.action: Hold the sustained block at even power - no surging over the top, no coasting.
skill_focus.why: Steady output keeps the DFA a1 R-R signal clean and makes the power-duration read trustworthy; surging corrupts both.

skill_focus.action: Stay aero-comfortable and breathe in rhythm; relax hands, jaw, shoulders.
skill_focus.why: Wasted tension raises HR and RPE without raising useful power, and noisy breathing muddies the a1 read.

mental_focus.intention: Read your engine honestly - not a hero effort, not a soft one.
mental_focus.why: A clean, controlled 28 min tells us exactly where FTP sits; burying yourself or backing off both waste the data.
mental_focus.reset: If you start fighting the bike, exhale, drop to 250 W, re-settle for a minute, then continue at the steady end.

pre_interval_cues: First 45 min smooth and steady for the a1 read.
The block is a read, not a race - cap RPE at 7.
during_workout_cues: Last 5 min should match the first 5.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Hold the top of the range (260 W) for the full block; do not jump above 100% - that breaks the read.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Drop to 250 W and hold steady; a clean 28 min at 250 still gives a usable read. Stop the block if power fades hard or RPE hits 8-9, and note where it broke.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Held 250-260 W for the full 28 min at RPE <=7 with no power fade in the last 5 min, smooth breathing throughout. That confirms 260. Fade or RPE 8-9 means the working FTP is closer to 250.
post_workout_capture: Did the last 5 min match the first 5
Highest steady power you could have held for the full block
Polar H10 worn the whole ride
